name: external_body_insert_no_key_guard
code:
proof fn phi_1_external_body_insert_no_key_guard(d: Directory, j: nat, base: nat, n: NodeEntry, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        j < d.num_entries(),
        d.update(j, n).inv(),
        d.interp_of_entry(j).insert(base, pte) == d.update(j, n).interp_of_entry(j),
        d.interp_aux(0).contains_key(base),
    ensures
        d.interp_aux(0).insert(base, pte) == d.update(j, n).interp_aux(0),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_insert_implies_interp_aux_insert(0, j, base, n, pte);
}
reason: The commented-out `!self.interp_aux(i).contains_key(base)` precondition means the `external_body` lemma claims insert works even when the key already exists elsewhere in the map — `Map::insert` overwrites, but `union_prefer_right` in `interp_aux` may re-shadow the inserted value from a lower-indexed entry

name: inv_allows_unaligned_base_vaddr
reason: `well_formed` has the alignment check on `base_vaddr` commented out, so directories with misaligned base addresses satisfy `inv()`

name: page_frame_base_unconstrained
reason: `pages_match_entry_size` checks `frame.size` but never constrains `frame.base` — a PTE can map to unaligned physical address 7, since `frames_aligned` is commented out in `inv()`

name: update_interp_insert_overwrites_existing
code:
proof fn phi_4_update_interp_insert_overwrites_existing(d: Directory, j: nat, base: nat, n: NodeEntry, pte1: PTE, pte2: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        j < d.num_entries(),
        d.update(j, n).inv(),
        d.entries[j as int].interp(d.entry_base(j)).insert(base, pte2) == n.interp(d.entry_base(j)),
        d.interp().contains_pair(base, pte1),
        pte1 != pte2,
    ensures
        d.update(j, n).interp()[base] == pte2,
{
    d.lemma_interp_entries_insert_implies_interp_insert(j, base, n, pte2);
}
reason: The external_body lemma allows insert to silently overwrite an existing mapping at `base` from any entry — without the `!contains_key` guard, a mapping from entry 0 can be overwritten by modifying entry j>0, violating the `union_prefer_right` priority where lower indices should take precedence

name: interp_aux_shadows_later_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_interp_aux_shadows_later_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        d.entries.len() >= 2,
        d.entries[0] is Page,
        d.entries[1] is Page,
        d.entry_base(0) == d.entry_base(1),
    ensures
        d.interp_aux(0)[d.entry_base(0)] == d.entries[0]->Page_0,
{
}
reason: `interp_aux` uses `union_prefer_right` giving lower-index entries priority — when two page entries compute the same base address, entry 0 silently shadows entry 1 with no disjointness check in the invariant
